titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in P60
P60
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2000 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. Further information about the transcription procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition of the majuscules at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Gap rest of verse tag removed at 17.3 by Rachel Kevern.
Gap tags and uncertain spaces at beginning of 16.22, beginning and end of 17.5, end of 18.29, end of 18.31, and end of 18.36 changed by Rachel Kevern.
[3][ed]εις[\ed] changed to [λαλ][ed]εις[\ed], [6] changed back to [λεγεις] at 16.29, [3]ται changed back to [εχε]ται at 16.33 and [5] changed to [ειπεν] at 18.4 by Rachel Kevern.
editio princeps tags reinstated around αι at 17.5 by Rachel Kevern.
editio princeps tags removed from αι at 17.5 by Rachel Kevern.
[λαλ][ep]εις[\ep] changed to [3][ep]εις[\ep] at 16.29 by Rachel Kevern.
[δεδωκας] changed to [6-7] and [δ]εδωκας changed to [0-1]εδωκας at 17.8. [εδωκας] changed to [6-7] at 17.24. [λεγει] changed to [5] at 18.4 by Rachel Kevern.
[λεγεις] changed to [6] at 16.29 and [εχε]ται changed to [3]ται at 16.33 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break in 17.18 corrected, [εξε] changed to [εχε] in 16.33 by David Parker.
Ghost page removed and ab/gap placement across page boundaries fixed by Catherine Smith.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 (apart from handling of ghost pages) by Hugh Houghton.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0 with manual adjustments.

body.note: This fragment can no longer be located. We were able to obtain a photograph of the lower part of it. We record the fragment as it is transcribed in the editio princeps.

ab.note: The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located on line 1. We record them here.
There are traces of ink on this line representing parts of some letters but their identification is not secure.
The editio princeps recorded two lines at the top of this fragment which cannot now be located.
There are traces of ink on this line representing parts of some letters but their identification is not secure.
The editio princeps recorded two lines at the top of this fragment, one at the end and letters throughout which cannot now be located.
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located on line 1. We record them here.
There are traces of ink on this line representing parts of some letters but their identification is not secure.
There are traces of ink on this line representing parts of some letters but their identification is not secure.
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located on lines 5 and 6. We record them here.
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located on this page. We record them here.
There are traces of ink on this line representing parts of some letters but their identification is not secure.
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located on this page. We record them here.
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located on this page. We record them here.
The editio princeps records letters which cannot now be located on this page. We record them here.
